source_jobs.title: Staff Product Engineer
source_jobs.description:
CAN YOU HELP US FIX THE WORLD'S MOST BROKEN SUPPLY CHAIN?

The fashion industry produces over 100 billion garments a year. 60% end up in landfill, not because people don't want these clothes, but because the supply chain for getting secondhand fashion from donation to resale is completely offline, manual, and broken.

Fleek is fixing this. Our mission is to make secondhand the first choice. We're building the infrastructure powering the global secondhand clothing trade, through a B2B marketplace and the AI systems digitising the supply chain behind it.

Through Fleek, vintage stores, online resellers, and retail buyers source graded secondhand inventory directly from wholesalers and rag houses across multiple countries. Our key enabler is FleekSort, an AI model fine-tuned on secondhand fashion, that can grade, price, and categorise items with just a photo, turning what has historically been an opaque and manual trade into a structured, searchable, and tradable global inventory layer.

Since 2022, we've saved 9 million items from landfill while tripling in size year-on-year. We're backed by some of the world's best investors, including a16z, Y Combinator, and HV Capital, alongside world-class angels who helped build Depop, Shopify, and Postmates.

Today, we serve thousands of resellers and retailers across the UK, Europe, and the US. With the secondhand fashion market projected to reach $350B by 2027, Fleek is building the AI-native operational and financial backbone for secondhand commerce globally.

We're looking for people who want to build something that matters, and move fast doing it. Sound good? Keep reading.




ABOUT THE ROLE

This is the most senior engineering role in the company. You will own Demand end-to-end, the product surface that drives revenue, growth, and customer love, across mobile, web, and backend. You'll lead a team of ~5 engineers in London, report into our CTO, Sanket Agarwal (ex-Google, Postmates), and partner deeply with Product and Design.

If it impacts buyer conversion, retention, or growth velocity, you own it.

Why This Role Stands Out

Demand is the most commercially important function at Fleek. You won't be a Staff engineer who advises from the sidelines, you'll own the entire system that converts supply into revenue.

Fleek is digitising an industry that has never had technology. There are no existing playbooks for building a transaction layer around secondhand fashion at wholesale scale, where every item is unique, inventory is unpredictable, quality is graded by AI, and the supply chain spans three continents. You will be designing how this market works.

Your systems will handle 500,000+ items a month today and millions by the end of the year. You'll work closely with our data and AI teams to build the services that connect FleekSort's classification engine to the marketplace experience. You'll set the technical standard for the entire engineering org.

For the first six months, you'll be deeply hands-on, writing production code, owning critical threads, and building context. As foundations stabilise, your impact shifts to direction-setting, multiplying team output, and building an engineering system that scales technically and organisationally. The goal is not to stop coding. The goal is to build an organisation that doesn't depend on you coding.




WHAT MAKES YOU A FLEEK STAFF ENGINEER?

 - System-Level Thinker: You see the full picture, from the UI a buyer touches, through the API, the data layer, and the infrastructure underneath. You make architectural decisions that compound speed, not just solve today's problem.

 - Product-Obsessed Leader: You think about why a buyer drops off at checkout, not just how the checkout service is architected. You instinctively connect technical decisions to commercial outcomes.

 - Builder Who Leads: You set direction by shipping, not by presenting. You write code, review code, and raise the bar through demonstration, not delegation.

 - Comfortable

source_jobs.title: Full Stack Finance Builder
source_jobs.description:
CAN YOU HELP US FIX THE WORLD'S MOST BROKEN SUPPLY CHAIN?

The fashion industry produces over 100 billion garments a year. 60% end up in landfill, not because people don't want these clothes, but because the supply chain for getting secondhand fashion from donation to resale is completely offline, manual, and broken.

Fleek is fixing this. Our mission is to make secondhand the first choice. We're building the infrastructure powering the global secondhand clothing trade, through a B2B marketplace and the AI systems digitising the supply chain behind it

Through Fleek, vintage stores, online resellers, and retail buyers source graded secondhand inventory directly from wholesalers and rag houses across multiple countries. Our key enabler is FleekSort, an AI model fine-tuned on secondhand fashion, that can grade, price, and categorise items with just a photo, turning what has historically been an opaque and manual trade into a structured, searchable, and tradable global inventory layer.

Since 2022, we've saved 9 million items from landfill and tripled in size year-on-year. We've raised ~$50M from a16z, Y Combinator, HV Capital, and others, and have recently closed our Series B. Our advisors and private investors include operators behind Depop, Shopify, and Postmates.

Today, we serve thousands of resellers and retailers across the UK, Europe, and the US. With the secondhand fashion market projected to reach $350B by 2027, Fleek is building the AI-native operational and financial backbone for secondhand commerce globally.

We're looking for people who want to build something that matters, and move fast doing it. Sound good? Keep reading.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re hiring a Full Stack Finance Builder to sit at the intersection of finance, product and engineering. This person will help Fleek automate finance workflows and accounting logic by turning business events into clean system behaviour, integrations and ledger outputs. It’s an unusually cross-functional role for someone who likes building real systems, not just analysing them.




WHY THIS ROLE STANDS OUT

This is not a standard finance systems role, and it’s not a standard software role either. The challenge is to understand how Fleek’s business actually works, how order states change, how cash moves, how capital gets allocated and then encode that into finance automation. That means building the connective tissue between the product, the data and the accounting layer.

What makes the role special is the blend. You need enough technical ability to build, enough product sense to understand what should happen and enough finance context to map business events into accounting outcomes. If you enjoy operating across domains and solving messy operational problems with systems, this is a rare opportunity to do it with real ownership.




WHAT MAKES YOU A FLEEK FULL STACK FINANCE BUILDER?

 - Technical First: You’re a builder at heart. You’re comfortable shipping integrations, stitching systems together and using code or automation to remove manual finance work.

 - Product-Minded: You don’t just build what’s asked. You want to understand the business event, the user flow and the operational edge cases before deciding what the right solution is.

 - Finance-Literate: You don’t need to be a career accountant, but concepts like ledgers, chart of accounts and accounting entries are familiar enough that you can build with confidence.

 - Data-Driven: You like working from underlying system logic and operational events not hand-wavy process maps.

 - Cross-Functional by Default: You enjoy working across finance, product and engineering and you’re energised by roles that don’t fit neatly into one box.
   


WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Automate Financial Reporting: Build systems that translate business events into financial reporting

 - Build Integrations: Connect Fleek’s internal systems and databases with tools to reduce manual workflows and improve finance accuracy.

 - Map Business Events to Le

source_jobs.title: Account Manager
source_jobs.description:
CAN YOU HELP US FIX THE WORLD'S MOST BROKEN SUPPLY CHAIN?

The fashion industry produces over 100 billion garments a year. 60% end up in landfill, not because people don't want these clothes, but because the supply chain for getting secondhand fashion from donation to resale is completely offline, manual, and broken.

Fleek is fixing this. Our mission is to make secondhand the first choice. We're building the infrastructure powering the global secondhand clothing trade: a B2B marketplace and the AI systems digitising the supply chain behind it.

Through Fleek, vintage stores, online resellers, and retail buyers source graded secondhand inventory directly from wholesalers and rag houses across multiple countries. Our key enabler is FleekSort, an AI model fine-tuned on secondhand fashion, that can grade, price, and categorise items with just a photo, turning what has historically been an opaque and manual trade into a structured, searchable, and tradable global inventory layer.

Since 2022, we've saved 9 million items from landfill while tripling in size year-on-year. We're backed by some of the world's best investors, including a16z, Y Combinator, and HV Capital, alongside world-class angels who helped build Depop, Shopify, and Postmates.

Today, we serve thousands of resellers and retailers across the UK, Europe, and the US. With the secondhand fashion market projected to reach $350B by 2027, Fleek is building the AI-native operational and financial backbone for secondhand commerce globally.

We're looking for people who want to build something that matters, and move fast doing it. Sound good? Keep reading.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We're looking for an Account Manager to own and grow a portfolio of Fleek's marketplace customers, driving retention, wallet share expansion, and long-term customer health.

You'll manage a book of resellers, vintage stores, and enterprise buyers across the UK and US, acting as their single point of contact while building the systems and processes that enable scalable, high-quality account management. Your customers range from online resellers and brick-and-mortar vintage shops to large-scale enterprises moving significant volume through Fleek.

This isn't a passive relationship management role. You'll be competing for wallet share against wholesalers from the Middle East, US, and Europe. You'll need to deeply understand your customers' businesses, proactively solve their problems, and find creative ways to grow their spend on Fleek month over month. The ideal candidate brings entrepreneurial energy, comfort with ambiguity, and an AI-first approach to scaling themselves and their portfolio.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own Your Portfolio: Manage a book of customers starting in the £5-15k monthly spend range, with a clear path to owning higher-value accounts as you ramp. Drive 10-20% month-on-month growth in retention and wallet share.

 - Build Healthy Customers: Ensure customers are spending on categories that make sense for our suppliers, with minimal refunds and cancellations. A healthy customer is a retained customer.

 - Drive Customer Engagement: Use WhatsApp, email, calls, stock lists, bundles, and handpicked supplier recommendations to keep customers active, ordering, and expanding their spend.

 - Win Back Lapsed Customers: Proactively identify and re-engage inactive accounts. Understand why they left and build compelling reasons for them to return.

 - Champion Self-Service: Act as the primary point of contact while actively encouraging and enabling customers to use the Fleek platform independently. Your goal is leverage, not dependency.

 - Build AI-Enabled Account Management: Work alongside the team to build AI-powered workflows that scale your ability to manage more accounts at higher quality. Automate the repeatable, personalise the meaningful.

 - Feed Insights Back: Work cross-functionally with product, operations, and supply to surface customer pain points (late deliveries, stock visibility, o

source_jobs.title: Senior Full Stack Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Can you help us fix the world's most broken supply chain?

The fashion industry produces over 100 billion garments a year. 60% end up in landfill, not because people don't want these clothes, but because the supply chain for getting secondhand fashion from donation to resale is completely offline, manual, and broken.

Fleek is fixing this. Our mission is to make secondhand the first choice. We're building the infrastructure powering the global secondhand clothing trade, through a B2B marketplace and the AI systems digitising the supply chain behind it.

Through Fleek, vintage stores, online resellers, and retail buyers source graded secondhand inventory directly from wholesalers and rag houses across multiple countries. Our key enabler is FleekSort, an AI model fine-tuned on secondhand fashion, that can grade, price, and categorise items with just a photo, turning what has historically been an opaque and manual trade into a structured, searchable, and tradable global inventory layer.

Since 2022, we've saved 9 million items from landfill while tripling in size year-on-year. We're backed by some of the world's best investors, including a16z, Y Combinator, and HV Capital, alongside world-class angels who helped build Depop, Shopify, and Postmates.

Today, we serve thousands of resellers and retailers across the UK, Europe, and the US. With the secondhand fashion market projected to reach $350B by 2027, Fleek is building the AI-native operational and financial backbone for secondhand commerce globally.

We're looking for people who want to build something that matters, and move fast doing it. Sound good? Keep reading.

About the Role

This is a Senior Full Stack Engineer role in Fleek's product engineering team, with a frontend lean. You'll report into our CTO, Sanket Agarwal https://www.linkedin.com/in/sanket-agarwal-32209641/ (ex-Google, Postmates), and work embedded with a product manager and designer in a small, senior squad focused on user growth and the core consumer experience.

Why This Role Stands Out

Fleek is building a social, video-first marketplace where every item is unique, supply is unpredictable, and the buying experience has to work for professional resellers making high-volume purchasing decisions and first-time buyers exploring a market they've never seen before.

You will be on our Growth team, rebuilding core user journeys from first principles, the discovery experience, the buying flow, seller onboarding, and the mobile app that's becoming an increasingly critical surface. You'll run your own experiments, interrogate your own data, and ship features that move metrics you can see. When you improve activation by 5%, you'll watch it in the revenue numbers the same week.

The platform handles 500,000+ items a month across web and mobile today and million+ by the end of the year, with AI-powered grading and pricing integrated throughout the experience. You'll work across both React (web) and React Native (mobile), and you'll be expected to reach into the backend when the feature demands it.

We're looking for a product engineer who thinks about user behaviour, commercial impact, and growth mechanics, and ships fast enough to test their hypotheses. You'll be expected to understand the business problems your code solves, and challenge whether we're solving the right ones.

What Makes You a Fleek Full Stack Engineer?

 - Growth-Wired: You think in funnels, activation loops, and conversion rates. You've shipped features that directly moved user acquisition, retention, or revenue, and you can point to the numbers.

 - Design Partner: You work with designers, challenge UX decisions, and suggest improvements based on what you see in the data. You care deeply about how an experience feels.

 - Customer-Focused Thinker: You've built for end users at scale. You understand how technical choices affect customers and the broader product experience.

 - Experiment-Driven: You run A/B tests, track results, and let data inform

source_jobs.title: GTM Engineer
source_jobs.description:
CAN YOU HELP US FIX THE WORLD'S MOST BROKEN SUPPLY CHAIN?

The fashion industry produces over 100 billion garments a year. 60% end up in landfill, not because people don't want these clothes, but because the supply chain for getting secondhand fashion from donation to resale is completely offline, manual, and broken.

Fleek is fixing this. Our mission is to make secondhand the first choice. We're building the infrastructure powering the global secondhand clothing trade, through a B2B marketplace and the AI systems digitising the supply chain behind it.

Through Fleek, vintage stores, online resellers, and retail buyers source graded secondhand inventory directly from wholesalers and rag houses across multiple countries. Our key enabler is FleekSort, an AI model fine-tuned on secondhand fashion, that can grade, price, and categorise items with just a photo, turning what has historically been an opaque and manual trade into a structured, searchable, and tradable global inventory layer.

Since 2022, we've saved 9 million items from landfill and tripled in size year-on-year. We've raised ~$50M from a16z, Y Combinator, HV Capital, and others, and have recently closed our Series B. Our advisors and private investors include operators behind Depop, Shopify, and Postmates.

Today, we serve thousands of resellers and retailers across the UK, Europe, and the US. With the secondhand fashion market projected to reach $350B by 2027, Fleek is building the AI-native operational and financial backbone for secondhand commerce globally.

We're looking for people who want to build something that matters, and move fast doing it. Sound good? Keep reading.




ABOUT THE ROLE

You'll join our GTM Acquisition team (new business) as our first GTM Engineer, reporting to our GTM lead. Your job is to build and scale the infrastructure we use to acquire the two kinds of customer we serve: online resellers (Depop, Vinted, Whatnot, eBay, Instagram shops) and physical vintage shops, from Brick Lane to Paris to New York.

Today, acquisition is manual and unscalable. This role exists to fix that. You'll build the custom tooling that turns one-off outreach into a repeatable engine: lead scraping, enrichment, outreach automation, sequencing, lead scoring, and honest funnel tracking, connected into our commercial operating system and the handoff to account management. The target is not incremental: we want this engine to take us to 500+ reseller closures.

This is a hybrid role for a rare profile: part sales engineer, part AI/automation builder. You take a problem, build something repeatable that handles it, ideally something an agent could run, then move on, rather than doing the same thing by hand every week.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Build the acquisition engine: design and ship the end-to-end system that sources, enriches, qualifies, and contacts prospects across both customer segments, replacing manual prospecting with repeatable infrastructure.

 - Automate sourcing and enrichment: build scrapers and enrichment pipelines across eBay, Depop, Vinted, Whatnot, and Instagram that surface qualified prospects with real signals: item volume, platform, geo, estimated spend, and store maturity.

 - Engineer outreach at scale: build multi-channel sequences (email, Instagram DM, WhatsApp, calls) that are personalised systematically by AI, not sprayed or hand-cranked, and designed around real constraints like Instagram's ~40 DMs per day cap.

 - Own the funnel: model and instrument every stage from sourced to reachable to contacted to replied to qualified to booked, with honest conversion assumptions, and use the data to decide where the engine leaks and what to fix next.

 - Design for two very different channels: online resellers are mostly Instagram-only, while physical shops have full contact details and can be grouped by city for visits. You'll build distinct playbooks for each and decide where AI does the work versus a person.

 - Build agents, not processes: wherev

source_jobs.title: Ex-YC Founder
source_jobs.description:
Reality check: Something didn't work out? We get it. We're looking for founders who still have that fire and want to join something that's still early but some things are already working.



Opportunity: We serve thousands of resellers and retailers across the UK, Europe, and the US. With the secondhand fashion market projected to reach $350B by 2027, Fleek is building the AI-native operational and financial backbone for secondhand commerce globally.

We've raised over $50M from a16z, Y Combinator, Burda Principal Investments, HV Capital, and others, and have recently closed our Series B. Our advisors and private investors include operators behind Depop, Shopify, and Postmates.



We are expecting that you:

 - Ran a YC company (obviously)

 - Know what worked and what didn't

 - Still want to build something massive

 - Care about applying AI for good for this world

 - Can wear multiple hats

 - Understand both technical and business sides



We're hosting our first-ever hackathon in partnership with Andreessen Horowitz https://www.linkedin.com/company/a16z/!

On Saturday, 11th July, we're opening the Fleek HQ doors in London for a one-day sprint to build the AI that makes circular fashion operate at scale. RSVP HERE: https://luma.com/usntwjdw

source_jobs.title: VP Finance
source_jobs.description:
Can you help us fix the world’s most broken supply chain?

The fashion industry produces over 100 billion garments a year. 60% end up in landfill — not because people don’t want these clothes, but because the supply chain for getting secondhand fashion from donation to resale is completely offline, manual, and broken.

Fleek is fixing this. Our mission is to make secondhand the first choice. We’re building the infrastructure powering the global secondhand clothing trade — through a B2B marketplace and the AI systems digitising the supply chain behind it.

Through Fleek, vintage stores, online resellers, and retail buyers source graded secondhand inventory directly from wholesalers and rag houses across multiple countries. Our key enabler is FleekSort — an AI model fine-tuned on secondhand fashion — that can grade, price, and categorise items with just a photo, turning what has historically been an opaque and manual trade into a structured, searchable, and tradable global inventory layer.

Since 2022, we've saved 9 million items from landfill while tripling in size year-on-year. We're backed by some of the world's best investors, including a16z, Y Combinator, and HV Capital, alongside world-class angels who helped build Depop, Shopify, and Postmates.

Today, we serve thousands of resellers and retailers across the UK, Europe, and the US. With the secondhand fashion market projected to reach $350B by 2027, Fleek is building the AI-native operational and financial backbone for secondhand commerce globally.

We’re looking for people who want to build something that matters — and move fast doing it. Sound good? Keep reading.

About the Role

The VP Finance will be a core part of Fleek’s leadership team, responsible for bringing financial discipline into the business to ready it for further rapid scale. This finance leader will build on and professionalise Fleek’s finance operations, thinking commercially across the business, and supporting on future fundraising.

What you’ll Own

 - Building, leading and developing Fleek’s finance function globally

 - Owning company-wide financial planning and analysis, cash flow forecasting, and budgeting

 - Instilling rigorous financial controls, hygiene, and compliance in a rapidly scaling organisation

 - Driving strategy alongside the founders by turning financial data into decision-making tools

 - Building the best payment experience for Fleek’s retailers and suppliers,

 - Maintaining a strong bottom-line and financial stability for the business

 - Leading and growing finance teams in Pakistan (fin-ops), India (accounting/reporting), and UK (commercial finance)

 - Supporting future fundraising efforts and investor relations

Who We’re Looking For

 - Experience in scaling the financial function from Series A through to Series C stage (or beyond)

 - Hands on leadership in finance strategy, controls, and operations

 - Ability to hire and retain world-class talent

 - Demonstrated ability to set up and scale core finance processes

 - Strong understanding of long cash cycles, forecasting, and working capital

 - Global finance experience - managing cross-border payments and multi-currency operations

 - Fintech knowledge beneficial (especially around credit, payment processing)

 - Strong commercial and product instincts - viewing Finance as a growth function

 - Previous experience in supporting on fundraising

 - Startup mentality and intellectual curiosity - thriving in high autonomy and ambition environments

 - Deeply analytical: data-driven decision making is second nature

 - Insatiably curious: you're constantly learning about new tools, approaches, and market changes

 - Hungry and ambitious: you're driven to stay relevant and push boundaries

 - Change agent: you've driven transformation in previous organizations

 - Hands-on: you're not afraid to roll up your sleeves and do the work yourself

How We Work

Fleek is an in-office company. We believe the best work happens when peo

Through Fleek, vintage stores, online resellers, and retail buyers source graded secondhand inventory directly from wholesalers and rag houses across multiple countries. Today, we serve thousands of resellers and retailers across the UK, Europe, and the US. With the secondhand fashion market projected to reach $350B by 2027, Fleek is building the AI-native operational and financial backbone for secondhand commerce globally.

We're looking for people who want to build something that matters, and move fast doing it. Sound good? Keep reading.

About the Role

Fleek is seeking a senior Commercial Director to lead the pivotal, demand-side revenue function, overseeing the acquisition and retention of its broad customer base. This is a genuine zero-to-one opportunity to build and scale a modern sales organization that blends traditional relationship management with cutting-edge AI automation. The Commercial Director will report directly to co-Founder Abhi Arora, and be the most senior sales/revenue role in the business.

You'll be taking ownership of customers spending £10K-£100K+ monthly on Fleek's groundbreaking platform, building systematic processes where currently ad-hoc approaches exist, and creating the playbook a modern, scaled commercial team operating in an AI-first world.

This role sits at the intersection of Fleek's evolving product strategy and its revenue engine. As Fleek transitions toward piece-level inventory and AI-enabled supply infrastructure, the Director of Commercial will be responsible for translating new product capabilities into scalable demand. This includes shaping sales and retention motions around new product primitives, defining how high-value buyers interact with a more dynamic and automated marketplace, and ensuring commercial growth scales in line with supply, unit economics, and operational realities.

What You'll Own

 - Lead the commercial team (~8 people across new business and account management functions)

 - Build the new business growth engine: create repeatable sales motions to drive top-of-funnel growth for high-value customers

 - Drive retention strategy for the best-performing customer cohort (currently at 3x higher retention than other segments)

 - Own key customer relationships and escalations (currently handled by the CEO)

 - Design and implement AI/agentic workflows to scale commercial operations without proportionally scaling headcount

 - Upskill and potentially expand the team with a focus on data-driven, tech-savvy commercial Talent

Who We're Looking For

 - Commercial management experience leading sales, account management, or revenue teams in a very high-growth environment

 - Marketplace experience (B2B or B2C), ideally from a fast-paced, metrics-driven environment

 - Expertise using data and metrics to support all key functional decisions and build business cases cross-functionally. You should live and

source_jobs.title: VP Engineering and Data

About the Role

To support our next stage of expansion, Fleek is seeking an experienced and ambitiousVP Engineering to lead and scale its Engineering organisation. The VP of Engineering will oversee a fast-growing engineering team, soon to be approximately 30 engineers, distributed across London and India, as well as manage Fleek's Data team, which is currently a team of four.

This is a senior leadership position reporting directly to Sanket Agarwal, Co-Founder ofFleek, and will play a critical role in shaping the company's technical direction, culture,and execution as Fleek scales.

What you'll Own

 - Lead delivery of Fleek's AI initiatives, which sit at the heart of the company's product and long-term strategy

 - Foster a culture of experimentation, agility, and hustle, encouraging rapid iteration and high ownership across teams

 - Build and evolve the engineering organisation structure, ensuring scalability as the company grows

 - Continue expanding the Engineering team, with the majority of hiring based in London

 - Own the Data function as it grows alongside the broader Engineering organisation.

 - Serve as a hands-on technical leader, able to build credibility quickly with engineers and guide architecture and execution

 - Attract, hire, and retain exceptional "A-class" talent, acting as a true talent magnet for the organisation

 - Partner closely with product and business leadership to ensure engineering teams are deeply aligned with company outcomes

Who We're Looking For

 - Experience leading engineering teams in high-growth, scaling environments

 - A background that began as a back-end engineer, with strong technical foundations

 - Proven ability to build engineering teams that are both product-focused and business-oriented

 - Previous experience overseeing data and managing data scientists

 - Experience working with or building AI-driven products

 - Strong leadership presence, with a track record of inspiring high-performing, fast-moving teams

 - Previous entrepreneurial experience in an ambitious tech-driven scale-up, or as a founder, would be advantageous

 - Experience in an international marketplace business is a strong plus for this role

source_jobs.title: VP People
source_jobs.description:
Can you help us fix the world's most broken supply chain?

The fashion industry produces over 100 billion garments a year. 60% end up in landfill, not because people don't want these clothes, but because the supply chain for getting secondhand fashion from donation to resale is completely offline, manual, and broken.

Fleek is fixing this. Our mission is to make secondhand the first choice. We're building the infrastructure powering the global secondhand clothing trade, through a B2B marketplace and the AI systems digitising the supply chain behind it.

Through Fleek, vintage stores, online resellers, and retail buyers source graded secondhand inventory directly from wholesalers and rag houses across multiple countries. Our key enabler is FleekSort, an AI model fine-tuned on secondhand fashion, that can grade, price, and categorise items with just a photo, turning what has historically been an opaque and manual trade into a structured, searchable, and tradable global inventory layer.

Since 2022, we've saved 9 million items from landfill and tripled in size year-on-year. We've raised ~$50M from a16z, Y Combinator, Burda Principal Investments, HV Capital, and others, and have recently closed our Series B. Our advisors and private investors include operators behind Depop, Shopify, and Postmates.

Today, we serve thousands of resellers and retailers across the UK, Europe, and the US. With the secondhand fashion market projected to reach $350B by 2027, Fleek is building the AI-native operational and financial backbone for secondhand commerce globally.

We're looking for people who want to build something that matters, and move fast doing it. Sound good? Keep reading.



About the Role

At Fleek, People is a growth function. The companies that win over the next decade will be the ones that figure out what it means to build, develop, and retain exceptional teams in an era where every employee is AI-enabled, where a single person can do what used to take five, and where the relationship between a company and its people is being fundamentally rewritten.

We don't think the old playbooks apply. Compensation, performance management, ways of working, career development, org design: versions of all of these exist, but they were built for a different era. At Fleek, they need to be reconceptualised from scratch for an AI-native company operating across three countries at speed.

You'll be building a globally connected People function from a standing start: the operating system that connects London, Karachi, and Bangalore, the frameworks that make every person at Fleek the best version of themselves, and the thinking that defines what a people function even looks like when AI changes everything about how we work.

This role reports directly to Abhi Arora, Co-Founder and CEO, and sits on the senior leadership team.



What You'll Own

 - Design and implement Fleek's operating system: ways of working, communication architecture, and decision-making frameworks across London, Karachi, Bangalore and beyond

 - Build the people function: org design, levelling, compensation philosophy, performance management, and the full employee lifecycle

 - Own talent density: set the hiring bar, invest in development, and build performance standards that make every person at Fleek a 10x version of themselves

 - Drive cultural coherence across three geographies with different working norms, expectations, and communication styles

 - Coach the founding team on people decisions, organisational design, and leadership development

 - Build an AI-native people function: use AI to create leverage across every people process rather than scaling headcount

 - Close compliance and risk gaps across multiple jurisdictions that currently create founder exposure (supported by HR specialists in region)

 - Set the company's operating cadence: the rituals, rhythms, and communication patterns that keep a fast-moving distributed team aligned



Who We're Looking For

 - 7+ yea

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Can you help us fix the world's most broken supply chain?

The fashion industry produces over 100 billion garments a year. 60% end up in landfill, not because people don't want these clothes, but because the supply chain for getting secondhand fashion from donation to resale is completely offline, manual, and broken.

Fleek is fixing this. Our mission is to make secondhand the first choice. We're building the infrastructure powering the global secondhand clothing trade, through a B2B marketplace and the AI systems digitising the supply chain behind it.

Through Fleek, vintage stores, online resellers, and retail buyers source graded secondhand inventory directly from wholesalers and rag houses across multiple countries. Our key enabler is FleekSort, an AI model fine-tuned on secondhand fashion, that can grade, price, and categorise items with just a photo, turning what has historically been an opaque and manual trade into a structured, searchable, and tradable global inventory layer.

Since 2022, we've saved 9 million items from landfill while tripling in size year-on-year. We're backed by some of the world's best investors, including a16z, Y Combinator, and HV Capital, alongside world-class angels who helped build Depop, Shopify, and Postmates.

Today, we serve thousands of resellers and retailers across the UK, Europe, and the US. With the secondhand fashion market projected to reach $350B by 2027, Fleek is building the AI-native operational and financial backbone for secondhand commerce globally.

We're looking for people who want to build something that matters, and move fast doing it. Sound good? Keep reading.



About the Role

This is a Senior Product Engineer (backend) role in Fleek's product engineering team. You'll report into our CTO, Sanket Agarwal (ex-Google, Postmates), and work embedded with a product manager and designer in a small, senior squad focused on the core marketplace experience.



Why This Role Stands Out

Fleek is digitising an industry that has never had technology. There are no existing playbooks for building an ecommerce experience around secondhand fashion at wholesale scale, where every item is unique, inventory is unpredictable, quality is graded by AI, and the supply chain spans three continents. You will be designing how the transaction layer works for a market that hasn't existed before.

You will be on our Growth team, owning the ecommerce journey from discovery to checkout, payment and credit systems, order management, and seller tools. Your systems will handle 500,000+ items a month today and million+ by the end of the year.

AI is at the core of everything we do, so you'll work closely with our data and AI teams to build the backend services that connect FleekSort's classification engine to the marketplace experience.

We're looking for a product engineer who can think about how systems affect buyers and sellers at scale. You'll be expected to understand the business problems your code solves, and challenge whether we're solving the right ones.



What Makes You a Fleek Product Engineer?

 - Product-Obsessed Builder: You think about what the seller sees when their inventory goes live, or why a buyer drops off at checkout. You instinctively connect technical decisions to commercial outcomes.

 - Comfortable with Ambiguity: We're building systems for workflows that didn't exist before Fleek. You thrive when the problem is undefined and collaboration is key.

 - Customer-Focused Thinker: You understand the dynamics of customer platforms and can understand how technical choices affect customers and our broader marketplace.

 - Owner, Not Renter: You take a feature from problem definition to production and monitor it afterwards.

 - Startup Pace: You've worked in environments where shipping matters more than perfection, where you deploy daily, and where velocity is a competitive advantage.



What You'll Do

 - Own the Ecommerce Journey: Design and build the backend systems powering Fleek's

source_jobs.title: Full Stack AI Builder
source_jobs.description:
CAN YOU HELP US FIX THE WORLD'S MOST BROKEN SUPPLY CHAIN?

The fashion industry produces over 100 billion garments a year. 60% end up in landfill, not because people don't want these clothes, but because the supply chain for getting secondhand fashion from donation to resale is completely offline, manual, and broken.



Fleek is fixing this. Our mission is to make secondhand the first choice. We're building the infrastructure powering the global secondhand clothing trade, through a B2B marketplace and the AI systems digitising the supply chain behind it.



Through Fleek, vintage stores, online resellers, and retail buyers source graded secondhand inventory directly from wholesalers and rag houses across multiple countries. Our key enabler is FleekSort, an AI model fine-tuned on secondhand fashion, that can grade, price, and categorise items with just a photo, turning what has historically been an opaque and manual trade into a structured, searchable, and tradable global inventory layer.



Since 2022, we've saved 9 million items from landfill while tripling in size year-on-year. We're backed by some of the world's best investors, including a16z, Y Combinator, and HV Capital, alongside world-class angels who helped build Depop, Shopify, and Postmates.



Today, we serve thousands of resellers and retailers across the UK, Europe, and the US. With the secondhand fashion market projected to reach $350B by 2027, Fleek is building the AI-native operational and financial backbone for secondhand commerce globally.



We're looking for people who want to build something that matters, and move fast doing it. Sound good? Keep reading.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We are looking for a deeply technical, hands-on Full-Stack Builder to embed across our AI and operations pods, building the agents, internal tools, and infrastructure that run Fleek end-to-end.



You will work alongside our CTO and Head of AI, jumping between teams to ship the systems that compound: agentic workflows that automate manual ops, internal tools that make every team 10x faster, and the connective tissue between our marketplace, our data, and our AI models. One week you might be building a sourcing agent for the commercial team. The next, productionising an integration with our warehouse partners. The next, hardening FleekOS — our internal operating system — so the next thing we build on it lasts.



This is the role for someone who has shipped real systems for 3-4 years, is bored of building the same thing in slightly different shapes at a big company, and wants range, ownership, and proximity to the part of AI that's actually useful right now: production agentic infrastructure inside a real business with real revenue.




WHAT MAKES YOU A FLEEK BUILDER?

 - Full-Stack in the Real Sense: You move comfortably across the stack. Backend services, frontend interfaces, integrations, data pipelines, and AI/agent infrastructure. You have favourite tools but no religion about them.

 - AI-Native: You use AI as your default toolkit, not a curiosity. You have built with LLMs in production, understand how agents actually work and break, and know the difference between a demo and a system that survives contact with real users.

 - Builder Mindset: You ship. You prefer working code over perfect plans, and you know how to scope something to ship this week rather than next quarter. You have opinions about what's worth abstracting and what isn't.

 - Pod-Hopper: You get energy from context-switching between problems. One week customs and ops, the next merchant tooling, the next internal infrastructure. You see the through-line.

 - Commercially Wired: You connect what you build to business outcomes. You can tell a good idea from a real one because you understand who the user is and what changes for them.

 - Comfortable with Ambiguity: Fleek is being built in real time. Specs are loose, the data is messy, and the right answer often isn't written down. You thrive there.

 - Action-Oriented

source_jobs.title: Organic Growth Lead, SEO & AI Search
source_jobs.description:
CAN YOU HELP US FIX THE WORLD'S MOST BROKEN SUPPLY CHAIN?

The fashion industry produces over 100 billion garments a year. 60% end up in landfill, not because people don't want these clothes, but because the supply chain for getting secondhand fashion from donation to resale is completely offline, manual, and broken.

Fleek is fixing this. Our mission is to make secondhand the first choice. We're building the infrastructure powering the global secondhand clothing trade, through a B2B marketplace and the AI systems digitising the supply chain behind it.

Through Fleek, vintage stores, online resellers, and retail buyers source graded secondhand inventory directly from wholesalers and rag houses across multiple countries. Our key enabler is FleekSort, an AI model fine-tuned on secondhand fashion, that can grade, price, and categorise items with just a photo, turning what has historically been an opaque and manual trade into a structured, searchable, and tradable global inventory layer.

Since 2022, we've saved 9 million items from landfill while tripling in size year-on-year. We're backed by some of the world's best investors, including a16z, Y Combinator, and HV Capital, alongside world-class angels who helped build Depop, Shopify, and Postmates.

Today, we serve thousands of resellers and retailers across the UK, Europe, and the US. With the secondhand fashion market projected to reach $350B by 2027, Fleek is building the AI-native operational and financial backbone for secondhand commerce globally.

We're looking for people who want to build something that matters, and move fast doing it. Sound good? Keep reading.


ABOUT THE ROLE

We treat SEO and AI Search (AEO/GEO) as one program, and we're hiring the person to own it end to end.

This is a senior IC role with full channel ownership. You'll be our first and only search expert: you set the strategy, build the pages, and own the number. You'll report into Growth and work alongside performance marketing, influencer and social, engineering, and a CX team sitting on a goldmine of real customer questions.

Organic is already our most efficient acquisition engine. Your job is to scale it, and to make Fleek the answer wherever second hand resellers and retailers search and ask questions, whether that's Google, ChatGPT, Perplexity, or AI Overviews.


WHY THIS ROLE STANDS OUT

The AI search playbook is being written right now, and almost nobody has run it with a real P&L attached. You'll get to.

This is also a builder's role, not a briefer's role. You own the website as a growth surface. You'll design and ship landing pages, and stand up programmatic templates from our own marketplace data. We care more about taste and what you can build than about how many slides you can produce. Engineering works with you on the deep technical lifts, but the day-to-day craft of the site is yours.


WHAT YOU'LL OWN

 - The number. New buyers from organic and AI search. You'll be accountable for growing organic at Fleek.

 - SEO and AI search strategy (AEO/GEO). Baseline and grow our share of voice in Google and LLM answers for the head questions we can't own outright ("best wholesale vintage supplier", "is Fleek legit"). These are won through earned mentions, not your own pages, so you'll build presence across the ecosystem LLMs actually cite: Reddit, YouTube, review sites, and roundups. You'll have our 1,000+ creator roster and UGC engine to deploy.

 - The website, as a product. You own how the site converts intent into buyers. You'll design and build landing pages, hub-and-spoke topic clusters, and the templates behind our programmatic pages. You're comfortable working directly in the website and shipping changes yourself rather than waiting on a queue. Good design instinct matters here: pages should be fast, clean, and genuinely useful to a savvy reseller.

 - Content across the full intent curve. Head questions won via citations and UGC. Middle-intent how-to guides (AI-assisted drafts, human-

source_jobs.title: Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
CAN YOU HELP US FIX THE WORLD'S MOST BROKEN SUPPLY CHAIN?

The fashion industry produces over 100 billion garments a year. 60% end up in landfill, not because people don't want these clothes, but because the supply chain for getting secondhand fashion from donation to resale is completely offline, manual, and broken.



Fleek is fixing this. Our mission is to make secondhand the first choice. We're building the infrastructure powering the global secondhand clothing trade, through a B2B marketplace and the AI systems digitising the supply chain behind it.



Through Fleek, vintage stores, online resellers, and retail buyers source graded secondhand inventory directly from wholesalers and rag houses across multiple countries. Our key enabler is FleekSort, an AI model fine-tuned on secondhand fashion, that can grade, price, and categorise items with just a photo, turning what has historically been an opaque and manual trade into a structured, searchable, and tradable global inventory layer.



Since 2022, we've saved 9 million items from landfill while tripling in size year-on-year. We're backed by some of the world's best investors, including a16z, Y Combinator, and HV Capital, alongside world-class angels who helped build Depop, Shopify, and Postmates.



Today, we serve thousands of resellers and retailers across the UK, Europe, and the US. With the secondhand fashion market projected to reach $350B by 2027, Fleek is building the AI-native operational and financial backbone for secondhand commerce globally.

We're looking for people who want to build something that matters, and move fast doing it. Sound good? Keep reading.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We're looking for an Account Executive to help define and then execute how we acquire our largest customers for the marketplace.



You'll need a genuine interest in fashion, bonus for secondhand and vintage, and the role centres on driving sales with vintage enterprises, brick and mortar stores, and resellers through visiting their stores, calling, emailing, understanding their needs, and building strong relationships.



The ideal candidate brings an entrepreneurial, creative approach to sales and relationship building. You'll be given a disproportionate level of responsibility and the opportunity to drive massive impact in this next phase of growth.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Acquire Key Accounts: Identify, prospect, and close Fleek's largest marketplace customers, vintage enterprises, retail stores, and high-volume resellers.

 - Build Relationships: Visit stores, get on calls, and build deep relationships with buyers. Understand their needs, pain points, and buying behaviour.

 - Define the Playbook: Help shape how Fleek acquires large customers. You're not just executing a sales process, you're building it.

 - Build an AI-Enabled Sales Engine: Work alongside our tech team to build AI-enabled and automated outreach and new business workflows.

 - Drive Revenue: Own and exceed targets. Take a proactive approach to pipeline building across online and offline channels.

 - Collaborate Across Teams: Work cross-functionally with product, operations, and marketing to feed customer insights back into the business and improve the platform.




WHAT YOU BRING

 - 2+ years of experience in a commercial role at an early-stage startup. You've sold in fast-moving environments where the playbook didn't exist yet.

 - Familiarity with online and offline sales and outreach channels. You know how to prospect, nurture, and close across multiple touchpoints.

 - Experience or appetite to build AI-enabled sales workflows alongside our tech team. You're excited about using AI to scale outreach and automate the new business engine.

 - Strong interpersonal and communication skills. You build and maintain relationships naturally. You're comfortable on the phone, in person, and in a cold outreach.

 - Self-motivated and entrepreneurial. You take a proactive approach to driving sales and consistently over-achie

source_jobs.title: Head of Controllership
source_jobs.description:
CAN YOU HELP US FIX THE WORLD'S MOST BROKEN SUPPLY CHAIN?

The fashion industry produces over 100 billion garments a year. 60% end up in landfill, not because people don't want these clothes, but because the supply chain for getting secondhand fashion from donation to resale is completely offline, manual, and broken.

Fleek is fixing this. Our mission is to make secondhand the first choice. We're building the infrastructure powering the global secondhand clothing trade, through a B2B marketplace and the AI systems digitising the supply chain behind it

Through Fleek, vintage stores, online resellers, and retail buyers source graded secondhand inventory directly from wholesalers and rag houses across multiple countries. Our key enabler is FleekSort, an AI model fine-tuned on secondhand fashion, that can grade, price, and categorise items with just a photo, turning what has historically been an opaque and manual trade into a structured, searchable, and tradable global inventory layer.

Since 2022, we've saved 9 million items from landfill and tripled in size year-on-year. We've raised ~$50M from a16z, Y Combinator, HV Capital, and others, and have recently closed our Series B. Our advisors and private investors include operators behind Depop, Shopify, and Postmates.

Today, we serve thousands of resellers and retailers across the UK, Europe, and the US. With the secondhand fashion market projected to reach $350B by 2027, Fleek is building the AI-native operational and financial backbone for secondhand commerce globally.

We're looking for people who want to build something that matters, and move fast doing it. Sound good? Keep reading.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re hiring a Controller to build the next layer of Fleek’s finance function. This is a senior individual contributor role sitting within Finance, focused on creating a scalable controllership foundation in a business that has grown fast and now needs stronger process, rhythm, and control. You’ll work closely with leadership and cross-functional teams to design how accounting should operate as Fleek scales.




WHY THIS ROLE STANDS OUT

At most companies, a Controller steps into an established finance function and improves it incrementally. At Fleek, you’ll be doing something much more interesting: building controllership in a company where the business has real scale and complexity, but parts of the accounting operating model still need to be designed from the ground up. In practice, that means you won’t just maintain processes, you’ll challenge them, rebuild them and create the operating cadence the company grows into.

This role is especially compelling for someone who has seen what “good” looks like in a larger or more mature environment, but also knows how to operate in the messiness of an early-stage company. Some systems already exist here. Some may need to be rethought entirely. The opportunity is to bring judgment, structure, and momentum to help establish the finance discipline that underpins the next phase of Fleek’s growth.




WHAT MAKES YOU A FLEEK CONTROLLER?

 - Builder Mentality: You don’t just inherit processes, you redesign them when they’re not fit for purpose. You’re excited by the idea of creating a controllership function, not just running one.

 - Comfortable with Ambiguity: You know how to operate when the company isn’t at zero-to-one, but the finance function partly is. Existing processes don’t intimidate you, even when they need to be torn down and rebuilt.

 - High Agency: You don’t wait for perfect alignment or clean inputs. You can drive an agenda, create momentum and push important operational changes through the organisation.

 - Influence Through Credibility: You can create buy-in outside finance, whether that’s around month-end discipline, information flows or better controls. People trust your judgment because it’s practical and grounded.

 - Scale-Informed: You’ve seen systems and accounting environments at scale, and you know how to apply t

notes: Fleek is a startup in the fashion industry, building a B2B marketplace and AI systems to digitize the supply chain for secondhand clothing.
